---
title: "Ollama: Run LLMs Locally"
author:
  - name: ML+X Community

date: 2026-02-24
date-format: long
image: "../../../images/vincent-van-zalinge-mDohQISBnCk-unsplash.jpg"

categories:
  - GenAI
  - LLM
  - Foundation models
---
## About this resource
[Ollama](https://ollama.com/) is a free, open-source tool that makes it easy to run large language models locally on your own machine. It handles model downloading, configuration, and serving with a simple command-line interface — no cloud services or API keys required. Ollama supports a growing library of open-weight models including Llama, Mistral, Gemma, Phi, and many more.

Ollama is particularly valuable for researchers who need to experiment with LLMs without sending data to external services, making it ideal for work with sensitive or proprietary data.

#### Key features
- **Simple setup**: Install and run models with a single command (e.g., `ollama run llama3.2`).
- **Model library**: Access a curated library of popular open-weight models, with new models added regularly.
- **Local and private**: All inference happens on your machine — no data leaves your computer.
- **API server**: Exposes a local REST API compatible with the OpenAI API format, making it easy to integrate with existing tools and code.
- **Cross-platform**: Available for macOS, Linux, and Windows.

#### GenAI use at UW-Madison
{{< include ../../includes/genAI-disclaimer.qmd >}}

#### High-level tips
- **Hardware requirements**: Most models require a machine with a decent GPU for reasonable performance. Smaller quantized models (7B parameters and below) can run on consumer hardware.
- **Quantization**: Ollama serves quantized versions of models by default, balancing performance and quality.
- **Integration**: Use Ollama as a backend for tools like [Open WebUI](https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui) for a ChatGPT-like interface, or integrate with Python via the `ollama` package.

## Questions?
If you have any lingering questions about this resource, feel free to post them on the [ML+X Nexus Q&A](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/ML-X-Nexus/discussions/categories/q-a) on GitHub.

## See also
- [**Model**: Llama (Meta)](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Toolbox/Models/Llama.html): Meta's open-weight LLM family, one of the most popular models to run with Ollama.
- [**Tool**: NotebookLM](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Toolbox/GenAI/NotebookLM.html): A cloud-based GenAI summarization tool for working with research papers.
- [**Tool**: GenAI at UW-Madison](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Toolbox/GenAI/GenAI-at-UW-Madison.html): Overview of enterprise GenAI tools supported by UW-Madison.
